danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct sprite animations for hostile creatures per planet
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Planet-specific visual obstacles (e.g., caustic terrain on Effluvia, jungle barriers on Sauria)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage zones requiring navigation skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Complete screen inversion with player and enemies drawn upside-down on Turvia
danger_signals.meaning: Inverted gravity mechanics requiring reoriented spatial awareness

danger_signals.visual_form: Missing or disabled Compumind visual presence on each planet
danger_signals.meaning: Objective incomplete; danger persists until Compumind located and restored

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual item pickup representing zone-specific power container
Colored armor visual variants (pink, green, white armor types)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary damage mitigation; defensive state enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Laser Sword, Power Pistol, Vertigun, Maxigun, Super Sword visual representations
Spacecraft environment for planet selection and teleport navigation
safety_signals.meaning: Safe hub space; mission planning area outside hostile planets

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Compumind visual representation as recoverable objective entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Compumind terminals/structures on each of eight planets; unique per planetary environment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kakos visual presence as discovered Compumind companion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Initial Compumind location; narrative anchor for mission briefing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Represented through shutdown Compuminds and planetary defense systems
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile creature designs specific to each planet reflect villain's biotechnology control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Planetary corruption/hazard manifestations; environmental obstacles as antagonist's presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Planet environment rendered with active hazards and enemies present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile creatures actively patrol/attack; environmental hazards remain active threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Compumind visual disappears from level; planet environment transitions to cleared state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy activity ceases or reduces; hazards may become navigable without active threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Complete 180-degree screen inversion; character and enemies drawn upside-down; falling becomes upward motion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gravity mechanics inverted; player must reorient spatial logic; enemies follow inverted gravity rules

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Technological aesthetic; digital/neon-inflected visual design differentiating from organic planets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Technology-themed enemies; electronic hazards; futuristic obstacle patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Organic green-dominant color palette; vegetation obstacles; natural terrain hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Biological enemies; natural environmental mechanics; climbing/navigation challenges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Caustic visual palette; degraded/corrupted terrain; trash/pollution visual indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Toxic damage zones; pollutant-based hazards; environmental contamination mechanics

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of eight planets (Cyberia, Sauria, Effluvia, Turvia, etc.) has visually distinct environment design communicating world identity and hazard type through color, terrain, and visual theme

environmental_storytelling.technique: Compumind visual placement as objective markers
environmental_storytelling.example: Each planet's Compumind location serves as primary visual goal; its presence/absence communicates mission status through environmental placement
Turvia's upside-down screen presentation communicates the planet's unique physics property without explicit text; visual design conveys mechanical difference
Hostile creatures designed per-planet reflect the villain's biotechnology control and planetary corruption; enemy visual diversity communicates world-state
Energy containers and armor pickups distributed across levels; visual presence/absence communicates resource availability and danger progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Melee-focused combat animation; lightsaber-style attack visual
Ranged attack animations; projectile spawn visual distinct from melee
Sprite rendered upside-down; jump direction inverted visually; falling reverses to upward movement
Visual sprite change reflecting armor type (pink/green/white); damage absorption animation distinct
Routine movement pattern animation; no attack behavior
Attack animation triggered; projectile or melee visual indicates threat activation
Enemy sprite rendered upside-down; movement physics inverted; behavioral adaptation to gravity state
Static visual presence; pickup-ready animation or glow indicating objective status

notes: The Adventures of Rad Gravity employs planet-specific visual design as primary world-state communication mechanism. The game's most distinctive narrative visual is Turvia's inverted gravity rendering, which communicates mechanical uniqueness through pure visual design. Faction communication is minimal; the game presents a binary opposition (protagonist + allied Compuminds vs. villain's biotechnology) communicated through environment design rather than explicit faction markers. The game rewards exploration and bug-testing through visual/mechanical discovery, suggesting intentional communication through unexpected visual states. Manual context (mentioned in TCRF source) indicates additional narrative support through external storytelling, but core world-state communication relies on environmental aesthetics and enemy/obstacle design.
